address: Phahonyothin Road, Wiang, Muang Chiang Rai

description.en:
## Overview

The Chiang Rai Night Bazaar is the city's main evening market and social hub, located on Phahonyothin Road in the center of town. While smaller and more laid-back than its Chiang Mai counterpart, this market has a charm of its own. It combines shopping, street food, and live entertainment in a compact, walkable area that feels genuinely local rather than overly touristy. Most evenings you will find a mix of Thai families, backpackers, and day-trippers winding down their temple tours over cheap eats and cold drinks.

## History

The Night Bazaar grew alongside Chiang Rai's tourism boom in the 1990s and 2000s as the city positioned itself as a gateway to the Golden Triangle region and the hill-tribe villages of the far north. The market serves as a commercial outlet for the handicrafts produced by the Karen, Akha, Hmong, and other ethnic communities living in the mountains surrounding the city. What started as a modest collection of stalls has evolved into a well-organized nightly market with permanent structures, a covered food court, and a performance stage.

## What to See

Hill-tribe vendors sell hand-woven scarves, intricate silver jewelry, and bamboo crafts made in the surrounding villages. You will also find wood carvings, marble sculptures, paintings by local artists, and the usual Thai souvenirs like elephant pants and Muay Thai shorts. The covered food court is a highlight, offering inexpensive Thai dishes like Pad Thai, Khao Soi, grilled meats, and fresh fruit shakes at prices well below restaurant rates. A central stage frequently hosts live music ranging from Thai folk songs to covers of Western pop, plus occasional traditional Thai dance performances.

## Practical Info

The bazaar is open nightly from around 6:00 PM to 11:00 PM and entry is free. Most vendors accept only cash, so bring Thai baht in small denominations. Prices for handicrafts can usually be bargained down by 10 to 15 percent, especially later in the evening. Weekend evenings, particularly Saturday, tend to be the liveliest. The market is centrally located and within walking distance of most hotels in the Wiang district. Allow 1 to 2 hours for browsing, eating, and catching a performance.
description.nl: Lokale nachtmarkt met handwerk straatvoedsel en culturele optredens

best_time_to_visit: Weekend evenings (especially Saturday) for the liveliest atmosphere
tips: Bring cash in small bills as most vendors do not accept cards
Try the covered food court for cheap and tasty local dishes
Arrive early to grab a good seat near the stage for live music and dance performances
